preview_text:
The model favors Mexico heavily, assigning a 71% win probability compared to Czech Republic's 7%. The underdog faces a substantial deficit in the forecast, leaving only a narrow path to victory.

The model's spread reflects a lopsided matchup. Mexico's draw probability of 23% suggests the match could reach stalemate, but the contest is far from competitive—the 64 percentage point gap between Mexico's and Czech Republic's win chances indicates a heavily tilted affair. A Czech Republic upset remains a low-probability outcome in this framework.

The model and market diverge meaningfully on team strength. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both assign Czech Republic roughly three times the win probability the model does (21% and 19% respectively versus 7%), while marking Mexico closer to even money (52% and 54% versus 71%). The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources. Both market venues align closely with each other on draw likelihood, near 27%.
